author: mark
text:
Federated-flip git hygiene: gitignore the .balls sidecars

When bl remaster --commit <url> flips a standalone repo to a master_url hub, state_repo::ensure swaps .balls/plugins/ for a symlink into the balls-owned hub clone. That left a tracked-but-deleted .balls/plugins/.gitkeep and an untracked, un-ignored .balls/plugins symlink that a careless git add -A would bake into the shared repo — a permanently noisy git status until the user manually git rm'd the gitkeep and hand-edited .gitignore.

The flip (and the seeded-master_url bl init path) now gitignore .balls/plugins, untrack the .gitkeep, and commit the whole transition, so it leaves a clean tree. bl remaster --detach reverses it: .balls/plugins leaves the ignore list and its .gitkeep is tracked again. .balls/plugins joins the bl-228d runtime-path table as a federated-only row, so the single source of truth still drives every consumer; adds a git_rm_cached helper.
